source: ASIC RG 261/262 + Farm Thru live $2-lead ad (LIVE-FMTH-NEVER-DONE) + PR #125 hypothesis validation 2026-04-28 + Motion Creative Benchmarks 2026
notes: Pre-campaign = before the Birchal offer document is live. Investment mechanics (dollar amounts, equity %, return projections) are PROHIBITED in ads per RG 261.96/99. The goal of pre-campaign ads is waitlist capture only — not conversion to investor. Every ad must include the general CSF risk warning (ADV-001, s738ZG(6)).

hook_weight_rationale.curiosity_gap_narrow_bound: Live $2-lead ad (LIVE-FMTH-NEVER-DONE) used narrow novelty + category + geography bound. PR #125 H1 confirmed: removing the geographic/category bound dropped composite 0.7926 -> 0.6053. Most powerful pre-campaign hook pattern observed to date.
hook_weight_rationale.ownership_invitation: PR #125 H2 confirmed: removing 'own a piece of' framing dropped composite 0.7926 -> 0.6053 — identical magnitude to removing the curiosity bound. Ownership identity beats investment transaction language.
hook_weight_rationale.price_anchor: BLOCKED for pre-campaign ads. ADV-002 prohibits specific investment amounts outside offer document. Do not use.
hook_weight_rationale.urgency: HIGH RISK in pre-campaign. Hard deadline scarcity ('last chance', 'closes in 24h') before an offer exists is misleading per ACL. Use soft future scarcity only.
hook_weight_rationale.founder_voice_build: PR #125 H3 DISCONFIRMED: replacing curiosity-gap with founder-voice alone dropped composite 0.7926 -> 0.6161. Founder voice is a support layer, not a replacement for the curiosity hook. Use founder voice in body, not opening line.

audience_motivation_hierarchy.ownership: Primary driver. 'Own a piece of' activates identity, not transaction. The reader wants to be a stakeholder in something they believe in. This is the lever that converts curiosity to action in pre-campaign.
audience_motivation_hierarchy.returns: Secondary. Relevant but cannot be stated in pre-campaign per ADV-002. Imply through outcome language ('pays farmers first', 'community-owned grocery model') — never project returns or cite percentages.
audience_motivation_hierarchy.novelty: Tertiary. 'Never been done in Australia' resonates because early-stage equity buyers self-identify as first-movers. Narrow the novelty: category + geography + mechanic, not generic 'disruption'.
audience_motivation_hierarchy.belonging: Quaternary. 'Join the people who eat with intention', 'FarmThru families' — activates community identity. Works best in body copy as proof layer, not hook.

allowed: we'll tell you the moment it goes live
leave your email — we'll tell you when
prohibited: only [N] spots left at this price
scarcity_conventions.rationale: Soft/future scarcity creates anticipation without implying a deadline that does not yet exist. Hard scarcity before an offer document is live risks misleading conduct (ACL s18). ASIC also views manufactured urgency in CSF ads as a trust-damaging signal per RG 261.

pre-campaign.outcome_statement_template: Leave your email — we'll tell you the moment it goes live.
pre-campaign.rationale: CTA must name the action AND its consequence. 'Join the Waitlist' alone scores lower than 'Join the Waitlist' + outcome body sentence (PR #125 seed vs alternatives; cta_clarity dimension). The consequence anchors the reader's expectation and reduces friction.

during-offer.compliance_gate: ADV-001 required on all. ADV-002 prohibits specific $ amounts in ads.
during-offer.note: Different playbook applies. Refer to compliance_rules.json.

csf_placement.rule: Asterisked footnote in the final paragraph only. Never in body sentences.
csf_placement.canonical_phrase: Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
csf_placement.placement_guidance: The compliance line dampens momentum when it appears mid-ad. Final-paragraph placement satisfies ADV-001 while preserving body copy flow. LIVE-FMTH-NEVER-DONE placed it as a starred line after 'Leave your email' — this pattern scores 5/5 on csf_placement dimension.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Narrow geographic + category bound is load-bearing in the curiosity hook
confirmed_hypotheses.test: PR #125 hook_swap: removed 'in Australia' + grocery category framing
confirmed_hypotheses.implication: Lead with the bound, not the proof. 'Never been done with a grocery store in Australia' outperforms generic 'revolutionary new model'.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Ownership framing ('own a piece of') is load-bearing
confirmed_hypotheses.test: PR #125 hook_swap: replaced ownership language with investment/return framing
confirmed_hypotheses.implication: Every pre-campaign FMTH ad should contain at least one ownership phrase in the first two paragraphs.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Founder voice alone could replace or lift the curiosity hook
confirmed_hypotheses.test: PR #125 hook_swap: led with founder voice, buried curiosity element
confirmed_hypotheses.result: DISCONFIRMED — composite dropped 0.7926 -> 0.6161 (-0.1765). Curiosity gap > founder voice as opening gambit.
confirmed_hypotheses.implication: Use founder voice in body ('we've built', 'we're about to') not as the opening line. Curiosity gap must come first.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Soft scarcity ('opens soon', 'first access') is load-bearing
confirmed_hypotheses.test: PR #125 hook_swap: scarcity language is in locked body — could not isolate cleanly
confirmed_hypotheses.result: INVALID PROBE — scarcity is body-level, hook_swap only tests hook. Could not confirm or disconfirm.
confirmed_hypotheses.implication: Treat soft scarcity as best-practice until a body_swap probe is run. Do not remove it.

ownership_phrases: own a piece of the farm-direct grocery model
gold_standard_ad.outcome_cta: Leave your email — we'll tell you the moment it goes live.
gold_standard_ad.headline: Own a piece of an Aussie-first grocery model

top_visual_formats: Text-forward (highest hit rate ~12%, spend use ratio ~1.9 — Motion Benchmarks 2026)
anti_patterns: Birchal, equity%, return projections — prohibited pre-campaign
Hard scarcity ('last chance', 'act now') — misleading before offer exists
Founder voice as opening line — H3 DISCONFIRMED (curiosity gap outperforms)
Investment language in body ('invest from $X', 'earn returns') — reframe as ownership
Bolted-on investment tease ('be part of it') without ownership framing
